subject: science
description: Earth’s nonliving resources have specific properties. Soil is composed of pieces of rock, organic material, water and air and has characteristics that can be measured and observed. Use the term “soil”, not “dirt”. Dirt and soils are not synonymous. Rocks have specific characteristics that allow them to be sorted and compared. Rocks form in different ways. Air and water are also nonliving resources. Note: Rock classification is not the focus for this grade level; this is found in grade 6. At this grade, the observable characteristics of rocks can be used to sort or compare, rather than formal classification.

subject: science
description: Earth’s resources can be used for energy. Renewable energy resources, such as wind, water or solar energy, can be replenished within a short amount of time by natural processes. Nonrenewable energy is a finite resource, such as natural gas, coal or oil, which cannot be replenished in a short amount of time.

subject: science
description: Some of Earth’s resources are limited. Some of Earth’s resources become limited due to overuse and/or contamination. Reducing resource use, decreasing waste and/or pollution, recycling and reusing can help conserve these resources.

subject: science
description: All objects and substances in the natural world are composed of matter. Matter takes up space and has mass. Differentiating between mass and weight is not necessary at this grade level.

subject: science
description: Matter exists in different states, each of which has different properties. The most recognizable states of matter are solids, liquids and gases. Shape and compressibility are properties that can distinguish between the states of matter. One way to change matter from one state to another is by heating or cooling.

subject: science
description: Heat, electrical energy, light, sound and magnetic energy are forms of energy. There are many different forms of energy. Energy is the ability to cause motion or create change. The different forms of energy that are outlined at this grade level should be limited to familiar forms that a student is able to observe.

subject: science
description: Offspring resemble their parents and each other. Individual organisms inherit many traits from their parents indicating a reliable way to transfer information from one generation to the next. Some behavioral traits are learned through interactions with the environment and are not inherited.

subject: science
description: Individuals of the same kind of organism differ in their inherited traits. These differences give some individuals an advantage in surviving and/or reproducing. Plants and animals have physical features that are associated with the environments where they live. Plants and animals have certain physical or behavioral characteristics that influence their chances of surviving in particular environments. Note: The focus is on the individual, not the population. Adaptation is not the focus at this grade level.

subject: science
description: Plants and animals have life cycles that are part of their adaptations for survival in their natural environments. Worldwide, organisms are growing, reproducing, dying and decaying. The details of the life cycle are different for different organisms, which affects their ability to survive and reproduce in their natural environments. Note: The names of the stages within the life cycles are not the focus.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Events in local history can be shown on timelines organized by years, decades and centuries.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Primary and secondary sources can be used to show change over time.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Local communities change over time.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Physical and political maps have distinctive characteristics and purposes. Places can be located on a map by using the title, key, alphanumeric grid and cardinal directions.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Daily life is influenced by the agriculture, industry and natural resources in different communities.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Evidence of positive and negative human modification of the environment can be observed in the local community.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Systems of transportation and communication move people, products and ideas from place to place.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Communities may include diverse cultural groups.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Members of local communities have rights and responsibilities.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Individuals make the community a better place by taking action to solve problems in a way that promotes the common good.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Laws are rules which apply to all people in a community and describe ways people are expected to behave. Laws promote order and security, provide public services and protect the rights of individuals in the local community.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Governments have authority to make and enforce laws.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: The structure of local governments may differ from one community to another.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Line graphs are used to show changes in data over time.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Both positive and negative incentives affect individuals’ choices and behaviors.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Individuals must make decisions because of the scarcity of resources. Making a decision involves a trade-off.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: A consumer is a person whose wants are satisfied by using goods and services. A producer makes goods and/or provides services.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: A market is where buyers and sellers exchange goods and services.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: Making decisions involves weighing costs and benefits.

subject: social_studies
theme: Communities: Past and Present, Near and Far
description: A budget is a plan to help people make personal economic decisions for the present and future and to become more financially responsible.
